mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes. It leverages Kubernetes custom resources to automate configuration and management, making it easier to set up monitoring for applications. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes resources and potential complexity in advanced configurations.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for the Prometheus Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Prometheus Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Prometheus Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace monitoring

helm install prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.75.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the monitoring stack.
> Installs the kube-prometheus-stack with a specific version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Prometheus Operator and its components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

kubectl get svc -n monitoring

```

> Lists all pods in the monitoring namespace to verify they are running.
> Lists all services in the monitoring namespace.

steps.title: Create a Prometheus custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a basic Prometheus custom resource to start monitoring.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 400Mi
EOF

```

> Creates a Prometheus instance with specified resources.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 400Mi
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Prometheus custom resource
steps.description:
This step checks if the Prometheus instance is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring

```

> Lists all Prometheus instances in the monitoring namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Prometheus Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Prometheus Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall prometheus-operator --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> Uninstalls the Prometheus Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the monitoring namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Prometheus Operator
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your current configuration, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml

helm upgrade prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.76.0

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Backs up the current Prometheus configuration.
> Upgrades the Prometheus Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating service account'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account required for Prometheus Operator is missing.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl create serviceaccount prometheus-operator -n monitoring' to create the service account.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus pod is in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the Prometheus pod.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests in the Prometheus custom resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the API server is down.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly configured and the API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Alertmanager is not receiving alerts
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Alertmanager configuration is incorrect or missing.

**Fix:**
Check the Alertmanager configuration and ensure it is correctly set up in the custom resource.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom alerting rules and improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update alerting rules to the new format as per the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Prometheus instance running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
